name: TAO Range Failure Reacceptance v1
edge_mechanism: failed breakout or breakdown back into balance
thesis: TAO 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing range-failure reversal after a multi-bar breakout loses acceptance and re-enters balance. The edge is not trend prediction; it is monetizing failed directional acceptance back toward value in a chop-heavy tape.
invalidation_condition: Abort if failed range escapes are too sparse, if reacceptance bars do not travel enough after costs, or if TAO behaves as persistent breakout tape instead of rejection-repair tape.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A push outside short-term balance loses acceptance, price re-enters value, and mean reversion carries far enough to monetize the failed escape.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Chop-heavy 4h conditions should generate more failed escapes than clean persistent breaks. TAO should express those failures visibly enough to trade.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is appropriate because failed escapes and sharp repairs are amplified there, making the mechanism easier to falsify quickly.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If escapes keep running rather than failing, reacceptance entries will be steamrolled.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trigger with density but follow-through is shallow, reduce target ambition before changing the reacceptance trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A decent number of short-hold reversals with first-target harvesting doing most of the work.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h plus at least one cross-lane show credible density and positive post-cost value repair after failed escapes.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade mirage, or repeated reacceptance entries that instantly fail back out of balance.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: low; excursion and re-entry are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed with moderate ATR thresholds rather than rare extremes

validation_basket.why_included: Best expressive lane for failed-escape repair behavior in the current basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Repairs show real density and enough post-cost travel.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The setup becomes too noisy or only touches value without extension.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Visible failed escapes followed by sharp value repairs.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism generalizes to a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH preserves the repair shape with cleaner execution.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea only exists on TAO volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Smaller but cleaner range-failure repairs than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Stress-tests whether the mechanism survives on a thinner high-beta lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS still shows orderly reacceptance repairs with usable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Noise dominates and no orderly repair appears.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Messier but still recognizable failed escapes into balance repair.
